content.text: I found the ONNX scoring module and the public model class; next I’m inspecting the encoder implementation to see where `OrtSession` is owned and should be closed.
I found the ONNX scoring classes. Next I’ll inspect similar ONNX-based modules and the module’s test dependencies to mirror the project’s resource-management conventions.

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<project xmlns="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.0.0" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsi:schemaLocation="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.0.0 http://maven.apache.org/maven-v4_0_0.xsd">
    <modelVersion>4.0.0</modelVersion>
    <parent>
        <groupId>dev.langchain4j</groupId>
        <artifactId>langchain4j-parent</artifactId>
        <version>1.13.0-beta23-SNAPSHOT</version>
        <relativePath>../langchain4j-parent/pom.xml</relativePath>
    </parent>

    <artifactId>langchain4j-onnx-scoring</artifactId>
    <name>LangChain4j :: Integration :: ONNX Scoring</name>

    <properties>
        <ai.djl.version>0.33.0</ai.djl.version>
        <onnxruntime.version>1.22.0</onnxruntime.version>
    </properties>

    <dependencies>

        <dependency>
            <groupId>dev.langchain4j</groupId>
            <artifactId>langchain4j-core</artifactId>
            <version>1.13.0-SNAPSHOT</version>
        </dependency>

        <dependency>
            <groupId>com.microsoft.onnxruntime</groupId>
            <artifactId>onnxruntime</artifactId>
            <version>${onnxruntime.version}</version>
        </dependency>

        <dependency>
            <groupId>ai.djl</groupId>
            <artifactId>api</artifactId>
            <version>${ai.djl.version}</version>
        </dependency>

        <dependency>
            <groupId>ai.djl.huggingface</groupId>
            <artifactId>tokenizers</artifactId>
            <version>${ai.djl.version}</version>
        </dependency>

    </dependencies>

</project>

content.text: I’ve found the ONNX scoring model classes. Next I’ll add a temporary regression test that proves `OnnxScoringModel.close()` is missing/doesn’t propagate, then implement resource closing and rerun it.

content.text: I reproduced the issue with a focused regression test: `OnnxScoringModel` currently does not compile when `close()` is invoked, confirming the missing API. Next I’ll inspect the runtime `close()` signature and add the resource-release path.

content.text:
Implemented the fix for `OnnxScoringModel` resource cleanup.

Changes made:
- `OnnxScoringModel` now implements `AutoCloseable`.
- Added `OnnxScoringModel.close()`, which delegates to the underlying ONNX cross encoder.
- `OnnxScoringBertCrossEncoder` now implements `AutoCloseable`.
- Added `OnnxScoringBertCrossEncoder.close()`, which calls `OrtSession.close()` and wraps any `OrtException` in a `RuntimeException`.

Validation performed:
- Added a temporary regression test confirming:
  - `OnnxScoringModel` is assignable to `AutoCloseable`.
  - `close()` propagates to `OrtSession.close()`.
- Confirmed the test failed before the fix because `OnnxScoringModel.close()` did not exist.
- Confirmed the test passed after the fix.
- Removed the temporary test file as requested.
- Ran module compilation successfully:

```bash
./mvnw -pl langchain4j-onnx-scoring -DskipTests compile
```

No commits were created.
